resources.string: The sweet meat slowly spoils; the mouth opens again.

The feast smells of perfume, rot, spice, and vomit. Amethyst wine flows like liquid light, and the diners keep swallowing even as their bellies split. Dignity is gone. Speech is gone. Even the tongue is chewed away in search of a purer taste. Fullness is an insult, a locked door within the body. Bite through it. Drink past it. Let appetite become the only organ left.
